expectations.evidence: The text opens with 'Existing approaches to roof segmentation for solar potential estimation divide into two families, each with a structural limitation.' — no filler opener present.

Line 4–8 explicitly names 'rasterize airborne point clouds into a Digital Surface Model (DSM)' and later 'DSM-derived estimates carry systematic orientation errors' and contribution item 1 specifically says 'without labelled supervision or DSM rasterization'.

Lines 10–12 state methods 'depend on manually annotated training sets or hand-tuned building-specific parameters that do not transfer across cities without re-annotation.' Line 13–15 adds 'annotating thousands of rooftops per city is prohibitive'.

Lines 24–26 mention 'Actueel Hoogtebestand Nederland 3 (AHN3) dataset across three Dutch cities --- Rotterdam, Amsterdam, and Utrecht --- covering 12\,000 rooftops'. All three specifics (AHN3, three cities, 12,000 rooftops) are present.

No 'not only X but also Y' constructions appear. The three enumerated contributions are distinct and substantive (algorithm, validation, pipeline release), not a suspiciously balanced rhetorical triad added as filler.
A full scan of the text finds none of the adjectives 'novel', 'robust', 'comprehensive', or 'state-of-the-art' used anywhere.

The file uses LaTeX comment markers (%), a \begin{enumerate}/\end{enumerate} environment, \citep{} citation commands, and \, for a thin space in '12\,000', confirming it is LaTeX-formatted prose ready for insertion.
